```json
[
  {
    "text": "The framework uses a vision-language model (VLM) to produce reference-frame task grounding and primitive-level 2D keypoints from calibrated multi-view RGB images.",
    "topic": "Zero-Shot Long-Horizon Dexterous Manipulation"
  },
  {
    "text": "3D keypoints are generated by triangulating view-wise VLM groundings and using reference-view ray voting to find geometrically consistent candidates across neighboring views.",
    "topic": "Multi-View 3D Grounding"
  },
  {
    "text": "For tool-use tasks, the system retrieves an object-centric atomic action corresponding to the inferred skill category and aligns its stored 6D tool trajectory to the scene.",
    "topic": "Tool-Use Execution"
  },
  {
    "text": "The lifted grasp keypoint is expanded into a task-conditioned grasp affordance region, and feasible grasp-motion pairs are generated with an arm-hand motion generator.",
    "topic": "Dexterous Execution"
  },
  {
    "text": "Real-world experiments demonstrate improved 3D grounding accuracy and execution reliability over single-view RGB-D grounding and fine-tuned VLA baselines.",
    "topic": "Experimental Results"
  }
]
```
